title: Gallery placeholder/preview lost on Stop-driven loop re-fire (ordering race)
description:
Split from MPI-226. When the user presses Stop during an armed loop, the re-fired next generation runs (backend + eventually a finished card) but its LIVE placeholder card + streaming preview latents + status-bar N/M progress do NOT appear until it finishes. The gen after that one displays correctly. INVESTIGATION-FIRST — do NOT patch blind (MPI-226 burned 6 attempts on this).

ROOT (proven by instrumented logs, 2026-07-08): the gallery's placeholder-visibility model is coupled to `_firstRunningEntry()` (js/components/Blocks/MpiGalleryBlock/MpiGalleryBlock.js — `_placeholdersForFirst`, the `generation:started`/`generation:preview`/`_rebuildAfterEnd` handlers). During a Stop, `activeGenerations.cancel()` runs the store cancel SYNCHRONOUSLY, which fires the loop callback → `_onLaneDrain` → re-fire → new `activeGenerations.start()` + `generation:started(NEW)` — ALL before the OLD entry is marked `cancelled`/removed (activeGenerations.js:122-125). So for a synchronous window BOTH old and new entries are status:'running', old is insertion-order-first. The decisive log fact: for the Stop-refired gen, `grid.updatePreview tempId=<new> inCardMap=FALSE` for the ENTIRE run — its placeholder was never (or was mounted-then-immediately-wiped) in the grid `_cardMap`. `_rebuildAfterEnd` (fired on the OLD cancelled) races the NEW gen's own `started` mount and the ordering drops the NEW placeholder. The gen AFTER gets a clean `_rebuildAfterEnd` with `livePh` including it, so it shows.

THE FRAGILITY: every attempted point-fix (trust payload placeholder; queue-guard on first-running; always-mount; remove first-running from _rebuildAfterEnd; paint-by-own-tempIds) fixed one ordering permutation and exposed another. The real fix is almost certainly a REWRITE to an idempotent reconcile: on ANY gen lifecycle event, mounted placeholders = { all currently-owned status:'running' gens' placeholderGroup+extraPlaceholders }, computed from activeGenerations, with NO reliance on event ordering or a single 'first-running' owner. Grid `_rerenderJustified` is debounced 16ms and reuses cards by group.id (MpiGalleryGrid.js:1329 _getCardEntry) — reconcile must be safe under that.

STATUS-BAR TWIN: the 'N/M shows wrong max (5/5 for a 2-step SDXL)' symptom is the SAME root — overlapping gens' tqdm bars bleed into one tracker. commandExecutor.js stageProgress is per-gen (closure-local) but the SSE `/comfy/events/stream` frames carry NO prompt_id/gen_id filter (only engine), so a doomed gen's still-draining bars feed the re-fired gen's tracker; phaseProgress.js self-correct `if(_stage>_total)_total=_stage` then climbs → inflated max. Fixing the double-fire (MPI-226, shipped) removes MOST overlap; if 5/5 still recurs after that, add a gen-id/prompt-id filter to the SSE step-progress consumer (commandExecutor.js ~1346-1460) OR reset the tracker on a new-prompt boundary. Full map in research/.

NOT A DATA BUG: the gen completes and saves correctly; this is display-only. Lower severity than the shipped MPI-226 fixes.
